CHAPTER III HIGH-RISK AI SYSTEMS

Article 9
Risk management system
1. A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and
maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems. The risk management system shall
be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the
entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review.

Article 10
Data and data governance
1. High-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of
models with data shall be developed on the basis of training, validation and
testing data sets that meet the quality criteria. Data sets shall be subject to
data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose,
including examination in view of possible biases.

Article 14
Human oversight
1. High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including
with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively
overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use, including
the ability to intervene, override or stop the system.
